subject: Project Management
title: The Transcontinental Railroad
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Transcontinental Railroad usable.

hook.text: In 1863, two railroad companies raced toward each other across 3,000 km of wilderness.

buildup.text: Central Pacific built east from Sacramento. Union Pacific built west from Omaha.

discovery.text: Thousands of Chinese and Irish workers blasted tunnels through granite by hand.

twist.text: The government paid by the mile — so both companies laid unnecessary curves for more money.

climax.text: On May 10, 1869, a golden spike joined the rails at Promontory Summit, Utah.

punchline.text: Incentives shaped the project more than any plan.

quiz.question: Why did railroad companies sometimes lay unnecessary curves?
options: They were paid by the mile and wanted more money
